reasoning: Based on FIDE rankings as of March 1, 2025, Mariya Muzychuk was exactly #13 with a rating of 2490. During March, she actively competed in the strong Nicosia FIDE Women's Grand Prix, where she finished tied for 3rd with a score of 5/9 (+1). Given the high average rating of the field (approx. 2486), a 5/9 score will yield a net positive rating change (estimated around +4 to +5 Elo, pushing her to ~2495). This likely allows her to overtake entirely inactive players directly above her (such as Kashlinskaya at 2493) and buffers her against players closely following her (like Harika, who also scored 5/9 and will trace a similar rating boost but started 7 points behind). Therefore, for the short-term April resolution dates (April 6 and April 29), I have very high confidence she will maintain a rank of #13 or improve to #12. If the Wikipedia page lags and isn't updated by April 6, it will continue to list her exactly at #13, which also resolves YES. Over the medium-to-long term (June 2025 through March 2026), uncertainty climbs significantly. Mariya's overarching 5-year trend has been a slow decline from her peak (~2560 down to 2490), while a very strong generation of rising female juniors (e.g., Divya Deshmukh, Zhu Jiner, Lu Miaoyi) is climbing the ranks. Due to these factors and the inherent volatility around the #13 cutoff, my probability steadily decays towards 0.5 for the distant resolution dates.
